title: IELTSMockPlatformDataset
description: FINALIZED: flat, relational-DB-friendly schema (one record per section/part, matching Tests/Sections/Questions/VisualAssets tables with FKs). book_type carried on every test record so Academic vs General Training vs Trainer vs Official Guide is queryable without a join back to documents.

documents.description: One entry per source PDF. Established in Phase 0 triage.

subfolder.description: Original folder path under IELTS-PDFS, verbatim.

book_type.description: Required on every test record too (denormalized) so the relational Tests table doesn't need a join back to Documents for this filter.

is_digital.description: True = clean embedded text layer, extracted directly, no OCR.

existing_ocr_layer_quality.description: DECISION (this pass): every non-fully-digital file, including 19.pdf and all glyphless-font files, runs the full OCR/layout pipeline regardless of this value. Recorded for audit only, not used for routing logic in v1.

page_boundary_anomaly_flag.description: ADDED: OCR'd PDFs don't always have 1:1 text-page-to-visual-page correspondence (matches the 18gt.pdf irregular per-page word count we found independently). True means a content-length sanity check failed and page-range-based ingestion can't be trusted blindly for this file.

sections.description: Reading passage, Listening part, or Writing task prompt.

visual_asset_ids.description: Section-level visuals, e.g. the chart a Writing Task 1 prompt describes.

visual_assets.description: Flat registry of every cropped visual, referenced by id from sections/questions.

answer_key_source.description: Auto-QA in Phase 5 flags anything other than printed_key_found for review.

visual_asset_ref.description: FK -> VisualAsset.asset_id, if this question depends on a figure.

content_type.description: ADDED after blueprint review: Trainer and Official Guide books contain pedagogical boxes ('What is Listening Section 2?', vocabulary-matching drills) that are real content but not gradeable IELTS questions. Without this flag they'd either get force-fit into question_type incorrectly or silently dropped.

question_number_start.description: ADDED: use instead of a single question_number when a question spans a range.

question_number_end.description: Equal to question_number_start for normal single questions. Different when one scoring unit spans multiple numbers, e.g. Cambridge's 'choose TWO letters' format where Q17-18 share one answer pair (key notation '17-18.AE') rather than being two independent questions.

page_number.description: DEPRECATED alias for pdf_page_index, kept for backward compat during transition. New code should read pdf_page_index.

pdf_page_index.description: 0-indexed raw PDF page, from PyMuPDF. Matches the field added to per-page extraction in Phase 1.

printed_page_number.description: The page number actually printed on the page, from PyMuPDF's page-label reader. Null if the page has no visible printed number (e.g. some cover/front-matter pages). This is what a human reviewer or the test-taker UI should show, never pdf_page_index.

bbox.description: [x0, y0, x1, y1] in rendered-page pixel coords, for traceability back to the source page.

image_path.description: Path to cropped, full-resolution image file.

structured_data.description: Polymorphic by asset_type. Bar/line/pie: {series:[{label,points:[{x,y}]}]}. Table: {columns:[...], rows:[[...]]}. Flowchart/process_diagram: {steps:[{id,label,next:[id]}]}. Map: {labels:[{text,approx_location}]}. Null if extraction not attempted/failed.

review_flag_reason.description: Why Phase 5 auto-QA queued this item, if it was queued.

stripped_watermark_patterns.description: ADDED after corpus verification: pirate/redistributor watermarks are injected as real extractable text, not just visual stamps — confirmed 'thudang' on 183/183 pages of one file, '@cambridge_library' on two others. A denoising blocklist pass must strip these from body text before it reaches passage_or_prompt_text/prompt_text, and log what was removed here for auditability.

ocr_substitution_warnings.description: ADDED: confirmed real corruption pattern where diacritics get OCR'd into digits (verified 'kākāpō' -> 'kakap6' in the corpus) with a deceptively high confidence score since every other character is correct. Run a known bad-pattern list (macron vowels -> digits, common ligature failures) as a pass separate from the generic confidence_score, which won't catch this.
